settings.title: 2025 October 13: Catppuccin theme added
settings.description: A new theme, Catppuccin, has been added. It has three variants: Frappe, Macchiato, and Mocha. You can select it in Typomagical Colour > Theme variant. Setting Obsidian to Light Mode will use the Latte variant.

settings.title: 2025 October 13: Highlights use gradient by default
settings.description: Highlights now use a gradient background by default. You can revert to solid background using the Style Setting 'Solid background for highlights'.

settings.title: 2025 June 6: Font weights removed
settings.description: Most extra weights for Spectral and Alegreya Sans have been removed, leaving 400 and 700 for Spectral, 400 and 800 for Alegreya Sans. The slider setting remains for advanced users who are able to install the fonts manually.

settings.title: 2025 February 2: Blockquotes now work in Live Preview
settings.description: The 'big with top border' and 'neuomorphic soft shadows' options now work in Live Preview

settings.title: 2024 July 4: Source mode text size
settings.description: In source mode, set heading size to be the same as other text. Added a Style Setting to turn this off, under Typomagical Headings > Source mode heading size reset

settings.title: 2024 March 6: Body weights
settings.description: Since Obsidian allows Google fonts, I've reintroduced all font weights for Spectral and Alegreya Sans. I've also expanded the weight slider from 200–800 to 200–900, so that Alegreya Sans users can use the **Black** 900 weight available.

settings.title: 2024 March 4: Amiri returned
settings.description: Kepano has clarified that while Obsidian does ban network requests in themes, it makes an exception for requests from fonts.googleapis.com. Thus, Amiri is back in Obsidian, but requires an internet connection to work.

settings.title: 2024 February 25 : Typomagical now works 100% offline
settings.description:
Obsidian has required all theme developers to remove network requests from their themes. This is so Obsidian can be used 100% offline, and also for security reasons. To comply, Typomagical has removed some features. These include:
- Amiri font for titles
- Body text weight setting, only 400 and 700 will work

If you dearly miss these, please contact me by filing a Github issue.

settings.title: Title font
settings.description: Titles, or H1s, are special. Thus, they should look special. Choose a Title font that suits you!

settings.title: Title weight
settings.description: The boldness of H1 text. Amiri only has 400 and 700 available as I don't personally use it. Please file a Github issue if you require more weights.

settings.title: Title alignment
settings.description: The horizontal alignment

settings.title: Title gradient
settings.description: Apply a vertical gradient to titles

settings.title: Heading weight
settings.description: The boldness of headings. I recommend choosing something different from your Title weight, and Body weight

settings.title: Heading scale
settings.description: The rate of growth between text and heading sizes. No scaling means headings and body text are the same size. Blank means Typomagical will attempt to choose the right scale based on your screen size.

settings.title: Source mode heading size reset
settings.description: In source mode, by default Typomagical will set headings to be the same size as other text. Turn this on to go back to Obsidian's default behaviour.

settings.title: Heading line height
settings.description: Height of each line in headings. I recommend around 1.2. If you have Embedded Note Titles plugin, that setting overrides this.

settings.title: Heading ligatures
settings.description: Decorative conjoined characters, such as the "ffi" in "office" and "ft" in "often".

settings.title: Image captions
settings.description: Show captions underneath images using ![filename.jpg|caption]. Does not work for ![](url.jpg).

settings.title: Disable image zoom
settings.description: Disable click+hold to zoom on images

settings.title: Theme variant
settings.description: Palette swaps. Don't see one you like? Let me know on https://github.com/hungsu/typomagical-obsidian/issues

settings.title: Allow Background color override
settings.description: The next setting will work when this is on

settings.title: Background color
settings.description: Overwrite background color with one of your choice

settings.title: Customize each mode
settings.description: Select a color scheme for each mode

settings.title: Slide out duration
settings.description: Set to 0 to remove slide-out animation

settings.title: an intentional writing space
settings.description: see an issue? want to suggest a feature? visit us on github: <https://github.com/7368697661/ultra-lobster> this theme was made with 💖 by kneecaps.

settings.title: custom accent
settings.description: select from hand-picked & theme-adjusted accent colors

settings.title: enable custom workspace background
settings.description: ⚠️ currently only supported when using the 'abaddon glass' dark subtheme.

settings.title: custom background image url
settings.description: paste a direct image link wrapped in url(""). example: url("https://i.imgur.com/image.png")

settings.title: halo gradient
settings.description: enable a wip background animation inspired by soft grainy gradients and angel numbers

settings.title: new-tab empty state (gradient + lobster)
settings.description: applies the “working edits” style empty state when no tabs are open

settings.title: blurred status-pills bg
settings.description: blurs bg of statusbar pill option

settings.title: minimal properties card
settings.description: removes the glass slab styling from the properties card

settings.title: match inline tags
settings.description: makes property tags look like standard inline wireframe tags

settings.title: island pill statusbar
settings.description: seperates statusbar items into pills

settings.title: text adjuster
settings.description: 🚨 must be a whole / decimal number such as 1 or 1.1. further scale can be changed via slider in appearance

settings.title: top level lists
settings.description: enable list header demarking 🚨 does not work in reading mode

settings.title: header size arrays
settings.description: options for changing your header sizes

settings.title: disable h2 underline
settings.description: removes the gradient underline from h2 headings

settings.title: header 1 weight
settings.description: nightingale looks best at weight >500

settings.title: disable canvas blur
settings.description: removes the backdrop-filter blur effect from canvas nodes

settings.title: center canvas text
settings.description: center align text in canvas cards

💫 **Star the theme on Github: [Underwater on Github](https://github.com/Seniblue/Underwater)!** </br></br> ✨ Theme updates and new features: [Releases](https://github.com/Seniblue/Underwater/releases) </br></br> **💡** The theme looks nice with [Lexend](https://www.lexend.com/) font!
💡 You can tweak these values using theme variables (set in the previous "Theme Colors" section) like **`var(--pine)`** or color values like **`#8ec7e8`** (hex) or **`rgb(142, 199, 232)`** (rgb).

settings.title: Table header
settings.description: Doesn't apply to "minimal" table

settings.title: New tab image
settings.description: Looks better with a small image

settings.title: Specific font for headings
settings.description: Just write the name of the font, or leave it blank for default font

settings.title: Disable slight image animation on hover
Disable slight button zoom on hover
Allow properties to be visible in reading mode as well
Disable checkboxes
settings.description: Disable this if you are using your own implementation via a CSS Snippet.

settings.title: Disable checkbox click (allows you to use checkboxes as bullets)

settings.description: 💡 You can set a hotkey for *Full focus mode* by going to **`Hotkeys`** (in the app settings), looking for **`Style settings: Toggle 🎯 Full focus mode`** and assigning your custom hotkey!

settings.title: 🎯 Full focus mode
settings.description: Removes tab bar, status bar, scrollbar, file header and properties heading. You can enable/disable it with a hotkey.

🎨 If you're looking for a custom **background color**, you can go to *`✏️ Editor colors`* → *`Workspace background color`*. </br> You can also enable the background image setting below and adjust the opacity of your custom color.

settings.title: Enable a dotted background for EXCALIDRAW drawings
Disable CALENDAR styling
settings.description: Weekends are highlighted

settings.title: Enable seamless DATAVIEW tables
settings.description: Dataview tables have the same styling as markdown tables (note that you can also use the "seamless-dataview" cssclass)

settings.title: Disable WIDGETS styling
settings.description: Colorful and smaller widgets

settings.title: Disable POMODORO TIMER styling
settings.description: Round stroke

settings.title: Light Color Scheme
settings.description: Light Color Scheme picker

settings.title: Dark Color Scheme
settings.description: Dark Color Scheme picker

settings.title: Main Color
settings.description: The main color influencing the interface colors.

settings.title: Color Intensity
settings.description: How intense the main color appears across the interface. (Dark Theme only)

settings.title: Header Color Style
settings.description: How intense the main color appears across the interface.

settings.title: Accent Color Main Header
settings.description: Apply the user's accent color to H1 headers.

settings.title: Disable Compact Panels in the sidebar
Disable Status Bar fade until hover
Disable title style for H1 Headings
Accidental exit prevention for tabs
settings.description: Disables the exit button when hovering over inactive tabs.

settings.title: Active line highlighting
settings.description: Highlights the active line with slight backdrop for easier text navigation.

settings.title: Cleaner Bases display
settings.description: Hides the filter toolbar options for embedded Bases in Reading mode.

settings.title: Image dimming in Dark mode
settings.description: Reduces the brightness of images in Dark mode except when hovered.

settings.title: Improved pinned tabs appearance
settings.description: Forces pinned workspace tabs to the start of the tabs list, and hides their titles to save space.

settings.title: Turn off Electron corner-smoothing
settings.description: Enable to fix visual bugs with tab containers and other rounded shapes.

settings.title: Disable performance tweaks
settings.description: Deactivates special CSS hacks which typically improve editor performance.

settings.description: Select your preferred theme appearance

settings.description: Turn on if you want to hide the scrollbar

settings.title: Disable Vibrant Indent
settings.description: Turn on if you want to hide the vibrant indent

settings.title: Disable Alternative Checkboxes
settings.description: Turn on if you are using your own implementation via a CSS Snippet.

settings.title: Disable Vibrant Folders
settings.description: Turn on if you want to hide the vibrant folders

settings.title: Callout Style
settings.description: Select your preferred callout style

settings.title: Bold Color
settings.description: Select your preferred **BOLD** color

settings.title: Italics Color
settings.description: Select your preferred _ITALIC_ color

settings.title: Bold + Italics Color
settings.description: Select your preferred **_BOLD+ITALIC_** color

settings.description: Select your preferred highlight color

settings.title: Title size
settings.description: Font size of your level 1 headings (#), in ems

settings.title: Colour Scheme (Light Mode)
settings.description: The colours used in the Light Mode interface.

settings.title: Colour Scheme (Dark Mode)
settings.description: The colours used in the Dark Mode interface.

settings.title: Readable Line Length
settings.description: This is how wide a note will appear when Readable Line Length is turned on. For most themes, this value is somewhere around 700px.

options.label: Fully Hidden (will not show on hover; toggle using a hotkey or command palette)

settings.title: Infobox Header 6
settings.description: Style Header 6 to be smaller and have a pale background, like the headings inside a Wikipedia infobox.

settings.title: Infobox Width
settings.description: This will affect the width of all components styled like Wikipedia infoboxes. Depending on your settings, these may include Info callouts, frontmatter, and images.

settings.title: Don't Render Info Callout as an Infobox
settings.description: By default, the theme renders any Info callout as a Wikipedia infobox. Toggle this setting to render Info callouts like a regular callout instead.

settings.title: Image Placement
settings.description: Control where images are placed on the page

settings.title: Custom Image Width
settings.description: Examples - 300px, 9em

settings.title: Image Max Width
settings.description: Set a max width for images (percentage)

settings.title: Theme Documentation
settings.description: [Website](https://willemstad.cc)

settings.title: Theme Repository
settings.description: [GitHub](https://github.com/tingmelvin/willemstad-x)

settings.title: Mobile Support
settings.description: Please note that Willemstad is not optimised for mobile devices at the moment.

settings.title: macOS and iOS Support
settings.description: Please note that the maintainer of Willemstad does not have access to either a Mac and/or an iOS device, which have slightly different styling in the core Obsidian app. Therefore, the theme has not been ensured to work properly with macOS and iOS. Bug reports are however very welcome to resolve these issues.

settings.title: No Animations
settings.description: When enabled, this disables all animations in this theme.

settings.title: No Blur Backdrop Filter
settings.description: The blur filter is activated by default in Willemstad, and might cause slight performance degradation on less powerful devices. When enabled, this disables all blur backdrop filters set by this theme.

settings.title: Re-enable Shadows
settings.description: Willemstad removes all shadows in Obsidian by default. When enabled, this enables all shadows disabled by this theme.

settings.title: Hide Scrollbars
settings.description: When enabled, Obsidian’s non-native scrollbars in Windows and Linux are replaced with zero-width tracks, giving the interface a neater look.

settings.title: Hide Tooltips
settings.description: When enabled, this hides all tooltips that appear when hovering over buttons and other UI elements.

settings.title: Border Radius
settings.description: This setting controls the border radius of all elements in this theme, and/or are calculated from this value. The default value is 6px.

settings.title: Border Radius of Images
settings.description: Controls the default border radius for images throughout the theme. Accepts any valid CSS radius value (e.g., 8px, 0, var(--radius-m)). Set '0' if you do not want your images to take a border radius.

settings.title: Divider Width
settings.description: Controls the thickness of divider lines throughout the theme.

settings.title: Note on Global Typography
settings.description: This section controls global typography settings like font sizes, underlines, bold, and italic styles, which affects every element within Obsidian. For settings specific to the editor, such as Markdown headers and Canvas typography options, please see the **Editor** section.

settings.title: Typeface
settings.description: Typeface options for Willemstad. For CSSClass settings, please use their specific options in the specific CSSClass settings panel. Font settings set in core Obsidian (Appearance > Font) will override the settings set here.

settings.title: Font Typeface — Mermaid.js
settings.description: Changes font for the in-built Mermaid.js visualisations in the theme.

settings.title: Bold and Italic
settings.description: Controls options for bold and italic text across Obsidian.

settings.title: Font Weight Modifier of Bold Text
settings.description: Adjusts the font weight for bold text, on top of the usual font weight for normal text. The default is 200.

settings.title: Font Weight
settings.description: Controls the font weight for italic text. The default is 400 (normal).

settings.title: Underlines
settings.description: Options to shift underlines lower/increase underline spacing (to increase readability) or to retain it as per default.

settings.title: Skip Ink for Underlines
settings.description: Willemstad by default does not have a space in the underline (also known as "skip ink") between interfering descenders (the bottom portion of letters "g, j, q, p, y"). This option restores the skip ink, which is also the original option in Obsidian.

settings.title: Underlines Spacing — Normal Text
settings.description: Choose which underline spacing you would like your normal text to have.

options.label: 🔴 Standard (Classic Obsidian Core/Classic Shimmering Focus style)
🟢 Maximum (Willemstad style) — Does not cross descenders (the bottom portion of letters "g, j, q, p, y")

settings.title: Underlines Spacing — Hyperlinks
settings.description: Choose which underline spacing you would like your hyperlinks to have.

options.label: 🟢 Maximum (Willemstad style) — Does not cross descenders (the bottom portion of letters "g, j, q, p, y")

settings.title: Light Mode
settings.description: Options to manipulate colours in light mode. Click any of the headers within to reveal options to change their respective color palettes.

settings.title: --color-base
settings.description: Background and Text

settings.title: Presets for Base Lightness
settings.description: Choose the amount of contrast you would prefer between your background and text elements.

options.label: Writing Mode (Even Lower Contrast, Raises Base Tones)

settings.title: Presets for Base Tone (Chroma and Hue)
settings.description: Choose the color temperature and hue palette for your light theme. Some options are recommended with or require base lightness setting for better/true colours, please adjust your settings accordingly.

options.label: Borealis (also known as Blueberries from another farm)

settings.title: Enable User-Set Base Tone for Light Mode
settings.description: When enabled, this allows you to override preset base tones (chroma and hue) with your preferred one.

settings.title: Enable Chroma & Hue Delta (Gradient Effect)
settings.description: When enabled, this applies a range (delta) to the base chroma and hue, creating a subtle gradient effect across different UI layers. A delta of 0 results in no change. Please note that this effect is very subtle.

settings.title: --color-accent
settings.description: Accents and Interactives

settings.title: Enable User-Set Accents for Light Mode
settings.description: When enabled, this allows you to override preset accents with your preferred ones.

settings.title: A Brief Lesson on Colour Theory Before You Start
settings.description: Colours can be organized on a 360° hue wheel to create specific harmonies. Analogous colours, which are close neighbours on the wheel (typically 15-45° apart), produce a calm and gentle effect. For a more vibrant yet balanced feel, a triadic scheme uses three colours spaced exactly 120° apart. A split-complementary harmony offers high contrast with less tension by pairing one colour with the two neighbours of its direct opposite.

settings.title: Set Hue Range for Accents
settings.description: Adjust the hue shift to create different colour harmonies. 0° = monochromatic, 30-60° = analogous, 90° = tetradic, 120° = triadic, 150° = split-complementary, 180° = complementary.

settings.title: Set Lightness Range for Accents
settings.description: Control how much darker each accent becomes. Higher values create more contrast between accent variations. Recommended: 5-15% (0.05-0.15) for subtle differences, 15-25% (0.15-0.25) for distinct variations.

settings.title: Accent Color via Picker
settings.description: The primary accent color for light mode. This will be used to generate additional accent variations based on your hue and lightness shift settings.

settings.title: Set Accents via Text Field Instead
settings.description: Use a text field instead of a colour picker for your primary accent. Accepts any valid CSS colour format (e.g., hex, RGB(A), HSL, OKLCH, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Custom Accent Colour
settings.description: Overrides the default accent colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset accent. Accepts any valid CSS colour format (e.g., hex, RGB(A), HSL, OKLCH, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Enable Fully Custom Accents
settings.description: When enabled, lets you override the secondary and tertiary accent colours (--color-accent-1 and --color-accent-2) using the fields below.

settings.title: Custom Accent Colour — Secondary
settings.description: Overrides the default secondary accent colour. If left empty, falls back to the preset. Accepts any valid CSS colour format (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, OKLCH).

settings.title: Custom Accent Colour — Tertiary
settings.description: Overrides the default tertiary accent colour. If left empty, falls back to the preset. Accepts any valid CSS colour format (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, OKLCH).

settings.title: Rainbow Colours
settings.description: Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Cyan, Blue, Purple and Pink

settings.title: Presets for Rainbow Colours (Light)
settings.description: Presets might not cleanly fit into Obsidian's specific colour palette.

options.label: Inspired by Artists, Artworks and Architecture

settings.title: Enable User-Set Rainbow Colours for Light Mode
settings.description: When enabled, this allows you to override preset rainbow colours with your preferred colours. Rainbow colours not overriden will use the prevailing colour available in the preset.

settings.title: Custom Colour — Red
settings.description: Overrides the default Red colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Custom Colour — Orange
settings.description: Overrides the default Orange colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Custom Colour — Yellow
settings.description: Overrides the default Yellow colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Custom Colour — Green
settings.description: Overrides the default Green colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Custom Colour — Cyan
settings.description: Overrides the default Cyan colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Custom Colour — Blue
settings.description: Overrides the default Blue colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Custom Colour — Purple
settings.description: Overrides the default Purple colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Custom Colour — Pink
settings.description: Overrides the default Pink colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: --text-selection
settings.description: Text Selection and Highlights

settings.title: Presets for Selection Colour
settings.description: Choose a color for selected text.

settings.title: Enable User-Set Selection Colour for Light Mode
settings.description: When enabled, this allows you to override the preset selection colour with your preferred colour. If left empty, it will use the prevailing colour from the selected preset.

settings.title: Custom Selection Colour
settings.description: Overrides the default selection colour. If left empty, defaults to the preset colour. Accepts all valid CSS colour values (e.g., hex, RGB, HSL, var(--interactive-accent)).

settings.title: Enable Contrast
settings.description: When enabled, UI elements like the titlebar, ribbon, status bar, and side panes will use a contrasting colour scheme taken from the current mode's chroma and hue.

settings.title: Use Base Colours from Dark Mode
settings.description: Imports the base background and text colours from your dark mode settings to use for the contrasting UI elements. The base colours within your main editing workspace will not be affected.

settings.title: Use Accent Colours from Dark Mode
settings.description: Imports the accent colours from your dark mode settings to use for the contrasting UI elements. The accent colours within your main editing workspace will not be affected.

settings.title: Enable User-Set Colours via Template for Light Mode
settings.description: Enable this option to allow the use of a snippet, for specific preset variables which will customize colors for the theme's appearance in light mode. This will override the variables set within Style Settings.

settings.description: [Template File](https://willemstad.cc/Dev+Docs/Template+Snippet)
