settings.title: Revert to Default Startup Screen
settings.description: When enabled, this reverts the startup screen to the default Obsidian startup screen.

settings.title: Select Obsidian Startup Icon
settings.description: This changes the Obsidian icon displayed on the startup screen. The default is the 3D version of the current logo.

settings.title: App Titlebar
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control the titlebar.

settings.title: Use Accent Colour for Titlebar
settings.notes: (For Obsidian Frame and Frameless Mode only)
settings.description: When enabled, this changes the titlebar colour to use the accent colours.

settings.title: Titlebar Accent Colour
settings.notes: (For Obsidian Frame and Frameless Mode only; Requires 'Use Accent Colour for Titlebar' enabled)
settings.description: Select the specific accent colour to be used for the titlebar here.

settings.title: Disable Titlebar Control Buttons
settings.notes: (For Windows and Linux, and in Frameless Mode)
settings.description: When enabled, the three control buttons (minimise, maximise and close) on the right side of the titlebar will be omitted. Drag the left sidebar to move the Obsidian window around your screen, double-click to minimise/expand, and Alt-F4 (Windows) to close.

settings.title: Remove Willemstad Version Number Suffix
settings.description: When enabled, the Willemstad version number suffix is removed from the titlebar, from the end of the Obsidian version number.

settings.title: Status Bar
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control the status bar.

settings.title: Style of Status Bar
settings.description: Choose the format your status bar should take in Obsidian.

settings.title: Content Justification in Full Status Bar
settings.description: Choose the justification of the content in the full-sized status bar. The default option is right-aligned.

settings.title: Alignment of Mini Status Bar
settings.description: Choose the position and alignment you would like to use for the mini-sized status bar in Obsidian.

settings.title: Coverage of Full Status Bar
settings.description: Determine whether the Full Status Bar should be full-width, or whether the ribbon should be full-height.

settings.title: Disable Floating Status Bar
settings.description: By default, the status bar will float away from the bottom edge of the application. This option allows you to anchor the status bar to the bottom of the screen.

settings.title: Floating Bar Distance from Bottom
settings.description: Controls how far the floating status bar is from the bottom edge of the screen. The default value is 8px.

settings.title: Account for Status Bar in Workspace
settings.description: By default, Willemstad does not account for space required for the mini status bar in the workspace. When this option is enabled, additional padding will be added to selected workspace elements to account for the status bar.

settings.title: Frosted Glass Aesthetic
settings.description: When enabled, the status bar will adopt a frosted glass aesthetic.

settings.title: Blur Intensity of Frosted Glass Aesthetic
settings.description: Change the amount of blur/frost applied to the status bar. The original settings is 4px.

settings.title: Max Width of Status Bar
settings.description: Change the maximum width of the status bar. The original settings is 85%.

settings.title: Borderless Status Bar
settings.description: When enabled, the status bar will have no borders applied to it.

settings.title: Ribbon and Main Workspace Buttons
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control the general Obsidian app interface.

settings.title: Ribbon Style
settings.description: Choose which ribbon/sidebar/side docks style you would like to use for Obsidian.

settings.title: Disable Draggable Ribbon
settings.notes: (Disabled by default in Floating Ribbon Mode for usability reasons)
settings.description: Willemstad sets the ribbon as draggable by default. This option allows the user to disable this feature.

settings.title: Floating Ribbon Height, when not hovered
settings.description: Set the height of the tabs for the Floating Ribbon, when not hovered.

settings.title: Floating Ribbon Height, when hovered
settings.description: Set the height of the tabs for the Floating Ribbon, when hovered.

settings.title: Note on Floating Ribbon Width
settings.description: As the width of the floating ribbon is set via the original ribbon width, this is not controllable by its own. However, this can be changed via the icon sizes, paddings, and margins controls available below in Main Workspace Buttons.

settings.title: Enable Blur for Floating Ribbon
settings.description: Willemstad sets the ribbon as draggable by default. This option allows the user to disable this feature.

settings.title: Blur Intensity of Floating Ribbon
settings.notes: (Requires 'Enable Blur for Floating Ribbon' enabled; Requires 'Floating Ribbon' ribbon style enabled;                 Requires 'No Blur Backdrop Filter' deactivated)
settings.description: This option allows one to adjust the blur intensity of the floating ribbon.

settings.title: Disable Colour of Sidebar Toggle Buttons
settings.description: By default, Willemstad sets the sidebar toggle buttons to be coloured when the sidebars are expanded. This option allows the user to disable this feature.

settings.title: Change Width of Rectangle in Opened Sidebar Toggle Buttons
settings.description: Change how expanded the centre window should be on the sidebar toggle buttons. The original settings are 24%.

settings.title: Note on Ribbon Width and Header Height
settings.description: Willemstad controls both the ribbon width and the header height via the icon sizes, paddings, and margins controls available below. As such, the ribbon width and header heights are not directly controllable here.

settings.title: Note on Icon Sizes
settings.description: Willemstad uses complex math to control icon sizes, paddings, and margin throughout the app interface. You are recommended to stick to the suggested icon and padding sizes, and to use Ctrl (Windows and Linux) or Cmd ⌘ (Mac) + Plus/Minus-key (+/-) to zoom in/out.

settings.title: Icon Size — Sidebar Toggle Buttons
settings.description: The original settings are 18px.

settings.title: Icon Size — Header Buttons
settings.description: The original settings are 16px.

settings.title: Icon Size — Tab Buttons
settings.description: The original settings are 14px.

settings.title: Padding — Header Buttons
settings.description: The original settings are 6px. Math calculations sets the padding of tab and header buttons from this value.

settings.title: Margin
settings.description: This sets many margin values in the UI, and works because of math magic. The original settings are 4px.

settings.title: Hover Fraction for Buttons
settings.description: Set the magnitude in which the icons in the ribbon and main workspace buttons should increase in size when hovered over, with respect to the total button space. The default value is 0.25.

settings.title: Vault Profile
settings.description: Options to control the vault profile drawer at the bottom of the left sidebar.

settings.title: Vault Profile Background Colour
settings.description: Changes the background colour of the vault profile drawer.

settings.title: Tabs in Editor Workspace
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control the tabs in the editor workspace tab headers.

settings.title: Disable Pinning Tabs to Front
settings.description: By default, pinned tabs in Willemstad are pinned to the front. This option allows one to disable this feature.

settings.title: Tab Style
settings.description: Choose the visual style for the main editor tabs.

settings.title: Borderless Active Tabs
settings.description: By default, Willemstad's modern tabs have a border around active modern tabs. This option allows one to disable this feature.

settings.title: File Explorer
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control the file explorer/navigation sidepane.

settings.title: Spacing between Titles in File Explorer
settings.description: Adjusts the vertical spacing between navigation titles. The default is 2px.

settings.title: File and Folder Title Wrapping
settings.description: Controls how file and folder names wrap in the navigation pane. 'No Wrap (pre)' is the default Obsidian behavior.

settings.title: Sticky Folder Titles
settings.description: When enabled, folder titles in the File Explorer will stick to the top of the pane as you scroll.

settings.title: Disable Custom File Extension Icons in File Explorer
settings.description: Willemstad changes the file extension text in the file explorer to icons. Toggle this to disable the change and revert back to the Obsidian default.

settings.title: Navigation Header
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control items within the navigation header, which is generally located at the top of panes such as File Explorer and Bookmarks.

settings.title: Alignment of Nav Buttons
settings.description: Controls the alignment of the buttons (e.g., New Note, New Folder) in the navigation header.

settings.title: Modals
settings.description: Change the widths and heights of modals (the settings/community plugins/themes/Publish menus).

settings.title: Revert Community Modals to Default
settings.description: When enabled, this disables the theme's custom layout for the Community Plugin and Theme store modals, reverting them to the standard Obsidian appearance.

settings.title: Community Modals Container Readme Per-Row Items
settings.description: In the Readme for Plugins and Themes in their respective stores, there are buttons to, for example, disable and uninstall the plugin/theme. This option allows one to adjust how many items should appear per row. The original settings are 5 columns.

settings.title: Spacing between Settings Left Column Navigation Items
settings.description: Adjusts the spacing of the settings left column sections, in between 'Options', 'Core Plugins' and 'Community Plugins' segments. Accepts any valid CSS unit (px, em, rem, %, etc.).

settings.title: Add Border for Settings Left Column Navigation Items
settings.description: Adds a bottom border to the settings left column section titles (Options, Core Plugins, Community Plugins).

settings.title: Note on Typography
settings.description: Global typography settings (like global font sizes, underlines, bold, italic, and strikethrough) are controlled in the **Global Typography** section. Settings specific to the editor (like Header and Canvas font sizes) can be found within this Editor section.

settings.title: Note on Block Widths
settings.description: Block Widths are an optional feature and must be enabled to take effect. This feature is being progressively implemented and is currently available for **Bases**, **Tables**, **iFrames**, and **PDF Embeds**.

settings.title: Block Widths — Master Control
settings.description: Use this to master-control for all block width features.

settings.title: Document Titlebar
settings.description: Tweak the document titlebar.

settings.title: File Header Justification
settings.description: This option allows one to adjust the justification of the file header.

settings.title: Note on Stacked Tabs Text Direction and Alignment
settings.description: In order to keep the stacked tabs in their original position whilst changing their text direction, use the text alignment option and set it to the opposite of the original option. (Left > Right; Right > Left)

settings.title: Stacked Tabs Text Direction
settings.description: This option allows one to adjust the text direction of the stacked tabs.

settings.title: Stacked Tabs Text Alignment
settings.description: This option allows one to adjust the justification of the title of the stacked tabs.

settings.title: Codeblocks
settings.description: Options to change codeblock display.

settings.title: Codeblock Language Display
settings.description: Use the dropdown to choose a codeblock language display type.

options.label: Default (UI font, flushed at bottom-right of codeblock)
Old-Style Willemstad & Shimmering Focus (Monospaced font, top-right of codeblock)

settings.title: Active Line Highlight
settings.description: Options to change the active line highlighting in the editing modes.

settings.title: Disable Active Line Highlight
settings.description: Willemstad has active line highlights by default across the specific line you're editing on. When toggled, this disables the highlighting.

settings.title: Colour for Active Line Highlight
settings.description: Sets the base colour for the active line highlight. The theme will apply transparency to this colour. Accepts any valid CSS colour format (e.g., hex, rgb, oklch, var(--color-accent)). Please note that the input field is meant to be colour-responsive and mimics how the Active Line Highlight looks in the editor when in focus.

settings.title: Opacity of Active Line Highlight
settings.description: Controls the opacity of the active line highlight. Lower values are more transparent. The default is 0.25; you are recommended to set values below 0.5.

settings.title: Editor Pane Manipulation (Readable Line Lengths and Pane Margins)
settings.description: Options to change the line lengths and pane margin/padding of the editor.

settings.title: Custom Maximum Line Length for Readable Line Lengths
settings.description: Set the maximum line length. Accepts all valid CSS units. Requires Obsidian's own 'Readable Line Lengths' option toggled on for this to work.

settings.title: Pane Margins in Editing Workspace
settings.description: Controls the padding on all sides within the editor pane, creating more or less 'breathing room' around your content. Please note that this option might affect components such as header numbering and header collapse indicators. The default is 32px.

settings.title: Headers
settings.description: Options to change the headers.

settings.title: Add Header Number (H1-H6)
settings.description: When enabled, this will add the header number to the left side just outside of the header to enable easier identification.

settings.title: Coloured Headers
settings.description: Options for coloured headers.

settings.title: Note on Coloured Headers
settings.description: By default, Willemstad uses the accent colours (--color-accent, --color-accent-1 and --color-accent-2) to colour the background of the headers. You can customise these by enabling 'Custom Header Colours (Background Colours)'. For legacy users of Willemstad, the previous header styles are available under 'Old Willemstad Header Colours'. If you prefer no background colours but coloured header text, please toggle the 'No Background Coloured Headers' option.

settings.title: Note on Header Text Colours
settings.description: If the Background Coloured Headers are enabled, Willemstad sets the header text colours mathematically in accordance to the colours chosen for your headers.

settings.title: No Background Coloured Headers
settings.description: Headers with background colours are enabled by default in Willemstad. When enabled, this will remove the background colouring for headers, and also optionally allows you to set headers with coloured text.

settings.title: Custom Header Colours (Background Colours)
settings.description: When enabled, this will allow you to set custom header colours.

settings.title: Old Willemstad Header Colours
settings.description: When enabled, this will use the old Willemstad header colours. There are two options for the old header colours: the original Willemstad colours, and the old Willemstad-style Nord colours.

settings.title: Old Header Colour Palettes
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Old Willemstad Header Colours' enabled)
settings.description: Use the dropdown to choose either the original Willemstad colours, or the old Willemstad-style Nord colours.

settings.title: Custom Header Colour 0
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' enabled)
settings.description: This header colour is applied to H1 (and H4 headers, if 'Complete Header Colour Control' is not enabled).

settings.title: Custom Header Colour 1
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' enabled)
settings.description: This header colour is applied to H2 (and H5 headers, if 'Complete Header Colour Control' is not enabled).

settings.title: Custom Header Colour 2
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' enabled)
settings.description: This header colour is applied to H3 (and H6 headers, if 'Complete Header Colour Control' is not enabled).

settings.title: Complete Header Colour Control
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' enabled)
settings.description: When enabled, this will allow you to fully customise all header colours.

settings.title: Custom Header Colour H4
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This header colour is applied to H4 headers.

settings.title: Custom Header Colour H5
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This header colour is applied to H5 headers.

settings.title: Custom Header Colour H6
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This header colour is applied to H6 headers.

settings.title: Custom Header Text Colour H1
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This text colour is applied to H1 headers.

settings.title: Custom Header Text Colour H2
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This text colour is applied to H2 headers.

settings.title: Custom Header Text Colour H3
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This text colour is applied to H3 headers.

settings.title: Custom Header Text Colour H4
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This text colour is applied to H4 headers.

settings.title: Custom Header Text Colour H5
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This text colour is applied to H5 headers.

settings.title: Custom Header Text Colour H6
settings.notes: (Requires 'No Background Coloured Headers' disabled; Requires 'Custom Header Colours' and 'Complete Header Colour Control' enabled)
settings.description: This text colour is applied to H6 headers.

settings.title: Header Text Colours
settings.description: These settings allow you to set the color for each header level from the set accent and rainbow palettes, should you prefer coloured text. These settings will automatically show you the options for the current light/dark theme mode. You are able to set different header text colours for both modes without them affecting each other.

settings.title: Header Typography
settings.description: Provides detailed control over the font size, weight, and style for each header level.

settings.title: Header Hexes
settings.description: Options to change the header hexes (# at the start of a heading).

settings.title: Make Header Hexes Smaller
settings.description: Headers Hexes (#) in Obsidian take the same font size as the header text. This option makes them smaller.

settings.title: Size of Header Hexes
settings.description: Change the size of the header hexes, relative to the header text's own font size. The default is 0.7em.

settings.title: Colour of Header Hexes
settings.description: Use the dropdown to choose the colour of the header hexes. While text-faint is the default in Obsidian, this is harder to discern with coloured headers. Willemstad matches the header hexes colours to that of the header text.

options.label: Match Colour of Header Hexes to Header Text

settings.title: Empty State Screen
settings.description: Options to customise the empty state screen (the screen that is displayed when no note is opened).

settings.title: Revert to Default Empty State Screen
settings.description: When enabled, this reverts the empty state screen to the Obsidian default.

settings.title: Highlights
settings.description: Options to change highlighting colours.

settings.title: Remove Highlight Box Shadow
settings.description: By default, Willemstad extends highlights by adding box shadows for better visual appearance. This, however, interferes with highlighting plugins such as Highlightr. This option allows for the removal of the highlighting box shadows.

settings.title: Use Original Highlight Colours
settings.description: When enabled, this will disable the custom Willemstad highlight colours and use the original colours used for highlighting.

settings.title: Highlighted Text Colour
settings.description: Colour of the text highlighted — this defaults to black.

settings.title: Highlight Colour — default
settings.description: Default highlight colour (==)

settings.title: Highlight Colour — additional #1
settings.description: Colour of the highlight when you use the italics markdown syntax with the highlight markdown syntax (*==).

settings.title: Highlight Colour — additional #2
settings.description: Colour of the highlight when you use the bold markdown syntax with the highlight markdown syntax (**==).

settings.title: Highlight Colour — additional #3
settings.description: Colour of the highlight when you use the italics, bold and highlight markdown syntax together (***==).

settings.title: Highlight Colour (Old) — default
settings.description: Default highlight colour (==)

settings.title: Highlight Colour (Old) — additional #1
settings.description: Colour of the highlight when you use the italics markdown syntax with the highlight markdown syntax (*==).

settings.title: Highlight Colour (Old) — additional #2
settings.description: Colour of the highlight when you use the bold markdown syntax with the highlight markdown syntax (**==).

settings.title: Highlight Colour (Old) — additional #3
settings.description: Colour of the highlight when you use the italics, bold and highlight markdown syntax together (***==).

settings.title: Frontmatter/Properties
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control the frontmatter. Also known as 'Properties' in Obsidian parlance.

settings.title: No YAML Colouring
settings.description: Willemstad colours the frontmatter YAML by default in Source Mode. When enabled, the frontmatter YAML will not be coloured.

settings.title: Hide Properties in Reading Mode
settings.description: When enabled, this hides the entire Properties block when in Reading Mode.

settings.title: Hide Properties in Live Preview
settings.description: When enabled, this hides the entire Properties block when in Live Preview.

settings.title: Hide 'Properties' Title
settings.notes: (Reading View and Editing View Live Preview)
settings.description: When enabled, this hides the 'Properties' heading above the metadata frontmatter table in both Live Preview and Reading Mode.

settings.title: Move Properties to Top of Note in Reading View
settings.description: When enabled, this moves the Properties block to appear before the in-line note title in Reading Mode.

settings.title: Move Properties to Top of Note in Live Preview
settings.description: When enabled, this moves the Properties block to appear before the in-line note title in Live Preview.

settings.title: Revert 'Add Property' Button to Default
settings.notes: (Reading View and Editing View Live Preview)
settings.description: When enabled, this reverts the 'Add Property' button to its original appearance, with text and its default position.

settings.title: Revert Alias Pill Style to Default
settings.notes: (Reading View and Editing View Live Preview)
settings.description: When enabled, this reverts the styling for alias pills (and other multi-select items) to the Obsidian default.

settings.title: Embeds
settings.description: Options to control the appearance of embedded notes and files.

settings.title: Disable Seamless Embeds
settings.description: By default, this theme makes embedded notes seamless (no borders, padding, or scrollbars). When enabled, this reverts embeds to the standard Obsidian style, adding back borders and scrollbars.

settings.title: Enable Block Widths for iFrames
settings.description: Enables a modern, content-aware width system for iFrames (e.g. YouTube videos, Google forms). When off, tables will take Obsidian's original settings.

settings.title: iFrame Content Width Magnifier
settings.description: A multiplier to increase the base width of the iFrame content, with respect to the document's line lengths. Default is 1.5 (150%). This setting will hardly make a difference if Readable Line Lengths is turned off.

settings.title: iFrames Max Width Ratio (Live Preview)
settings.description: The maximum width the iFrame can occupy, as a ratio of the container's width. 1 = 100%. Default is 1.

settings.title: iFrames Max Width Ratio (Reading Mode)
settings.description: The maximum width the iFrame can occupy, as a ratio of the container's width. 1 = 100%. Default is 0.96.

settings.title: iFrames Aspect Ratio
settings.description: Sets the aspect ratio of the iFrame. Use the format 'width / height', and 'auto' for it to default to its original aspect ratio, which can be used alone, or added before or after to the 'width / height' format. Default is 'auto 16/9'.

settings.title: Enable Block Widths for PDF Embeds
settings.description: Enables a modern, content-aware width system for PDF embeds. When off, PDF embeds will have a simple, standard layout.

settings.title: PDF Embed Content Width Magnifier
settings.description: A multiplier to increase the base width of the PDF embed content, with respect to the document's line lengths. Default is 1.5 (150%).

settings.title: PDF Embed Max Width Ratio (Live Preview)
settings.description: The maximum width the PDF embed can occupy, as a ratio of the container's width. 1 = 100%. Default is 1.

settings.title: PDF Embed Max Width Ratio (Reading Mode)
settings.description: The maximum width the PDF embed can occupy, as a ratio of the container's width. 1 = 100%. Default is 0.96.

settings.title: Lists
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control user-created lists.

settings.title: Checkbox Radius
settings.description: This option allows one to change the radius of the checkbox in lists.

settings.title: Enable Alternative Checkboxes
settings.description: When enabled, this allows the usage of the Alternative Checkboxes Reference Set developed by Damian Korcz, also supported by other themes, in your lists.

settings.title: Tables
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control Markdown tables.

settings.title: Enable Block Widths for Tables
settings.description: Enables a modern, content-aware block width system for tables. When off, tables will take Obsidian's original settings.

settings.title: Disable Table Striping
settings.description: By default, Willemstad colours alternate rows differently in Markdown tables. Enable this should you prefer Obsidian’s default look for ordinary Markdown tables.

settings.title: Table Width Behavior
settings.description: Choose whether tables should shrink-to-fit their content (standard Obsidian behaviour) or be forced to fill the available width. The two Fit to Width options differ in their Live Preview look. Not requiring scrolling for the button is functionally sensible and aesthetically less pleasing, and vice versa.

options.label: Fit to Width (Add Table Column button does not require scrolling)
Fit to Width (Add Table Column button requires scrolling)

settings.title: Tables Content Width Magnifier
settings.description: A multiplier to increase the base width of the table content, with respect to the document's line lengths. Default is 1.5 (150%). This setting will hardly make a difference if Readable Line Lengths is turned off.

settings.title: Tables Max Width Ratio (Live Preview)
settings.description: The maximum width the table can occupy, as a ratio of the container's width. 1 = 100% (whole container width). Default is 1.

settings.title: Tables Max Width Ratio (Reading Mode)
settings.description: The maximum width the table can occupy, as a ratio of the container's width. 1 = 100% (whole container width). Default is 0.96 (96%).

settings.title: Bases
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control the Bases core plugin.

settings.title: Enable Block Widths for Bases
settings.description: Enables a modern, content-aware block width system for Bases.

settings.title: Revert to Standard Bases Appearance
settings.description: Reverts the Bases core plugin to its stock colour scheme instead of Willemstad’s palette.

settings.title: Non-Editable and Read-Only Bases in Reading Mode
settings.description: By default in Obsidian, Bases are editable in Reading Mode. Enable this to turn off editing in Reading Mode.

settings.title: Bases Content Width Magnifier
settings.description: A multiplier to increase the base width of the Bases content, with respect to the document's line lengths. Default is 1.5 (150%). This setting will hardly make a difference if Readable Line Lengths is turned off.

settings.title: Bases Max Width Ratio (Live Preview)
settings.description: The maximum width Bases can occupy, as a ratio of the container's width. 1 = 100% (whole container width). Default is 1.

settings.title: Bases Max Width Ratio (Reading Mode)
settings.description: The maximum width Bases can occupy, as a ratio of the container's width. 1 = 100% (whole container width). Default is 0.96 (96%).

settings.title: Canvas
settings.description: Options to manipulate items in the Canvas.

settings.title: Hide Node Labels
settings.description: When enabled, hides the labels/titles on individual nodes (files/cards) in the Canvas.

settings.title: Hide Group Labels
settings.description: When enabled, hides the labels on groups in the Canvas.

settings.title: Node Label Font Size
settings.description: Sets the font size for labels on individual nodes (files/cards). Accepts any valid CSS unit (e.g., 1em, 14px, 1.2rem).

settings.title: Group Label Font Size
settings.description: Sets the font size for labels on groups. Accepts any valid CSS unit.

settings.title: Canvas Background Colour
settings.description: Sets the background colour of the Canvas. Accepts any valid CSS colour format (e.g., hex, rgb, hsl, oklch, var(--some-variable)).

settings.title: Canvas Background Dot Colour
settings.description: Sets the colour of the dots in the Canvas background. Accepts any valid CSS colour format.

settings.title: Canvas Background Dot Size
settings.description: Controls the size of the dots in the Canvas background. Obsidian's default is 0.7. Change this to zero to get a Heptabase-like background.

settings.title: PDF Viewer
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control the PDF viewer. For PDF embeds, please refer to the Embeds section above.

settings.title: Invert PDF Colours in Dark Mode
settings.description: When in dark mode, this inverts the colors of embedded PDFs to make them easier to read. Please note that this option is very rudimentary and there are no good CSS options given the limitations of accessing colours within PDF in Obsidian. You are strongly recommended to use Obsidian's 'Adapt to Theme' option, accessible via the PDF toolbar, first, and only resort to this should it fail to work.

settings.title: Ancillary Editor Options
settings.description: Options to manipulate and control other aspects of the editor.

settings.title: Show URLs By Default
settings.description: Courtesy of Chris (Shimmering Focus), Willemstad has a feature that hides the URLs by default. When enabled, the URLs will be visible by default.

settings.title: Overflow Fix
settings.description: Courtesy of SlRvB (ITS Theme), this is a fix for possible overflow issues in the editor in Reading Mode, which is especially obvious when HTML Aside and Infobox are aligned to the left. When enabled, the overflow issue will be fixed.

settings.title: Note on Global CSSClass
settings.description: Please note that global CSSClasses (CSSClasses that affect the entire page, rather than just specific elements), are controlled in their separate Style Setting categories prefixed with 'Willemstad CSSClass'.

settings.title: For more information about each specific callout type, please refer to the documentation links below.
Global Metadata Classes
settings.description: Metadata classes can be appended to the end of a callout syntax to produce certain effects. Replace "metadata-classes" in the following syntax, which is copyable, to test things out.

settings.title: Documentation for Global Metadata Classes
settings.description: [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Callouts/Global+Metadata+Classes)

settings.title: Syntax for Global Metadata Classes
settings.description: >[!callout-class|metadata-class]

settings.title: Types of Global Metadata Classes
settings.description: no-t, no-title; opp, opposite; no-bg, no-background; no-s, no-style; mono, monospace; fl, float-l, float-left, left; fr, float-r, float-right, right; no-m-t, no-margin-top; no-m-b, no-margin-btm, no-margin-bottom; no-m-l, no-margin-left; no-m-r, no-margin-right;

settings.title: Standard Callouts
settings.description: To use standard callouts, use the following syntax below, which is also copyable for your own convenience. The types of standard callouts available in Willemstad is also listed below.

settings.title: Documentation for Standard Callouts
settings.description: [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Callouts/Callouts)

settings.title: Syntax for Standard Callouts
settings.description: >[!note]

settings.title: Types of Standard Callouts
settings.description: note; abstract, summary, tldr; info, todo; tip, hint; check, done; question, help, faq; warning, caution, attention; failure, fail, missing; danger, error; bug; example; quote, cite; file, attachment; phone; email, mail; goal; success; important; code; positive, plus; negative, minus

settings.title: Callout Style
settings.description: Choose the visual style for callouts. 'Willemstad' is the default theme style. To use Obsidian's standard styling, please use the 'Revent to Original Obsidian Styling on Standard Callouts' toggle below this option.

settings.title: Revert to Original Obsidian Styling on Standard Callouts
settings.description: Willemstad by default styles the callouts, toggle this in order to disable this and return to Obsidian's standard callouts appearance.

settings.title: Disable Callout Border Hover/Always Show Callout Border
settings.description: By default, the colored left border on callouts only appears on hover. When enabled, this makes the border permanently visible. If you do not want a border, toggle this to always show the border and set the callout border width below to zero.

settings.title: Callout Border Width (Left)
settings.description: Controls the additional thickness of the colored left border on callouts, added to the base width. Set this and the base width to zero to hide the border completely. Default is 5px.

settings.title: Callout Border Width (Base)
settings.description: Overrides the base border width for callouts. Accepts any valid CSS length (e.g., 1px, 0.25rem, 0) or variables that resolves to a length. The default is set in a way that the override will take the values of any presets set by the callout.

settings.title: Documentation for Infobox Callouts
settings.description: [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Callouts/Infobox)

settings.title: Syntax for Infobox Callouts
settings.description: >[!infobox]

settings.title: Title Size of Infobox Callouts
settings.description: Changes the font size of the infobox title, using the heading sizes defined in Willemstad.

settings.title: Maximum Width of Infobox Callouts
settings.description: Sets the maximum width of the infobox relative to the pane size. The infobox will not grow larger than this percentage.

settings.title: Ideal Width of Infobox Callouts
settings.description: Sets the ideal target width of the infobox. If this value is larger than the 'Maximum Width of Infobox Callouts', the maximum width will take precedence. You can use any valid CSS unit, such as 500px, 50%, 40vw, 30em, etc.

settings.title: Documentation for Aside Callouts
settings.description: [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Callouts/Aside)

settings.title: Syntax for Aside Callouts
settings.description: >[!aside]

settings.title: Float Aside Callouts Left
settings.description: The aside callouts float right by default. Toggle this option to change the float to left by default. You can float an aside left/right individually via global metadata classes 'fl', 'float-l', 'float-left' for left, and 'fr', 'float-r', 'float-right' for right.

settings.title: Documentation for Columns Callouts
settings.description: [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Callouts/Columns)

settings.title: Syntax for Columns Callouts
settings.description: >[!columns]

settings.title: Syntax for Columns Metadata Classes
settings.description: >[!columns|metadata-class] > >> [!callout-class|metadata-class]

settings.title: Types of Columns Metadata Classes
settings.description: w1, width-1; w2, width-2; w3, width-3; w4, width-4; w5, width-5;

settings.title: Top Margin of Column Callouts
settings.description: Sets the margin above children callouts to create vertical space between the children callouts and the column callout title. You can use any valid CSS unit, or valid variables, such as 20px, 1.5em, 2vh, etc.

settings.title: Default Title Display for Image Callouts
settings.description: Controls whether the title is globally hidden for [!images] callouts. To show/hide the title on a per-callout basis, please use the relevant metadata classes.

settings.title: Documentation for Gallery-style Image Callouts
settings.description: [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Callouts/Gallery)

settings.title: Syntax for Gallery-style Image Callouts
settings.description: >[!images|gallery]

settings.title: Image Border Radius in Gallery-style Image Callouts
settings.description: Controls the border radius of images inside gallery-style callouts. Accepts any valid CSS radius value (e.g., 0, 4px, var(--radius-m)).

settings.title: Documentation for Grid-style Image Callouts
settings.description: [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Callouts/Image+Grid)

settings.title: Syntax for Grid-style Image Callouts
settings.description: >[!images|grid]

settings.title: Image Border Radius in Grid-style Image Callouts
settings.description: Controls the border radius of images inside gallery-style callouts. Accepts any valid CSS radius value (e.g., 0, 4px, var(--radius-m)).

settings.title: Gap between Images in Grid-style Image Callouts
settings.description: Controls the gap/spacing between images within an image grid callout. Accepts any valid CSS length (e.g., 4px, 0.5em, 1rem).

settings.title: Documentation for Banner CSSClass
settings.description: [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Inline+CSSClass/Banner+CSSClass)

settings.title: Syntax for Banner CSSClass
settings.description: cssclasses: [banner]

settings.title: Banner Height
settings.description: Sets the height of the banner image. You can use any valid CSS unit (e.g., 200px, 25vh).

settings.title: Banner Bottom Margin
settings.description: Sets the margin below the banner image, creating space between the banner and the content below it. Accepts any valid CSS unit (e.g., 0px, 1em).

settings.title: Banner Gradient Mask
settings.description: Defines the linear gradient used to fade the bottom of the banner image. Modify this to change the fading effect.
settings.default: linear-gradient(to bottom, black 0%, black 50%, oklch(0 0 0 / 0.8) 75%, oklch(0 0 0 / 0.5) 80%, oklch(0 0 0 / 0.3) 85%, oklch(0 0 0 / 0.15) 90%, oklch(0 0 0 / 0.08) 95%, transparent 100%)

settings.title: Progress Bars
settings.description: To use progress bars, use the following syntax below, which is also copyable for your own convenience. Change the value of the progress bar by changing the value attribute. Change the max value by changing the max attribute.

settings.title: Syntax for Progress Bars
settings.description: <progress value="50" max="100"></progress>

settings.title: Use Original Progress Bar Styling
settings.description: Willemstad changes the default Progress Bar styling, however this is only appropriate for Progress Bars using a 0-100 scale. Toggling this would result in the original Progress Bar colour styling used instead.

settings.title: HTML Aside
settings.description: To use HTML Aside, use the following syntax below, which is also copyable for your own convenience. HTML Aside floats to the right by default, allowing you to add notes. Please note that markdown syntax DOES NOT work inside HTML Aside — instead, you must use HTML Syntax to reflect changes (for example, instead of **** for bold text).

settings.title: Syntax for HTML Aside
settings.description: <aside>text goes here</aside>

settings.title: Text Alignment for HTML Aside
settings.description: This option allows one to change the text alignment and justification of the HTML Aside. By default, the text is aligned to the left.

settings.title: Float Alignment for HTML Aside
settings.description: This option allows one to change the float alignment of the HTML Aside. By default, the aside is floated to the right. Should you choose to float it to the left, it is recommended to enable the "Overflow Fix" option.

settings.title: HTML Infobox
settings.description: To use HTML Infobox, use the following syntax below, which is also copyable for your own convenience. HTML Infobox is very similar to HTML Aside, except that the title in HTML Infobox is styled as well. Like in HTML Aside, markdown syntax DOES NOT work inside HTML Infobox — instead, you must use HTML Syntax to reflect changes (for example, instead of **** for bold text).

settings.title: Syntax for HTML Infobox
settings.description: <aside class=infobox><title>title goes here</title> text goes here</aside>

settings.title: Float Alignment for HTML Infobox
settings.description: This option allows one to change the float alignment of the HTML Infobox. By default, the infobox is floated to the right. Should you choose to float it to the left, it is recommended to enable the "Overflow Fix" option.

settings.title: Colour for Today's Date
settings.description: This option allows one to choose one of the accent colours for today's date in the Calendar plugin.

settings.title: Colour for Year
settings.description: This option allows one to choose one of the accent colours for the year in the Calendar plugin.

settings.title: Vertical Scroll Kanbans in Side Panes
settings.description: When enabled, multiple kanban lanes in the side panes can be navigated via scrolling vertically rather than scrolling horizontally.

settings.title: Fit to Width of Side Panes
settings.description: When enabled, the kanban lanes in the side panes will fit to the width of the side panes.

settings.title: Max Height of Vertical Kanban Lane in Side Panes
settings.description: Set the maximum height of each kanban lane in the side panes, when vertical.

settings.title: Vertical Scroll Kanbans in Main Workspace (Editor) Panes
settings.description: When enabled, multiple kanban lanes in the main workspace panes can be navigated via scrolling vertically rather than scrolling horizontally.

settings.title: Fit to Width of Main Workspace (Editor) Panes
settings.description: When enabled, the kanban lanes in the main workspace panes will fit to the width of the pane.

settings.title: Max Height of Vertical Kanban Lane in Main Workspace (Editor) Panes
settings.description: Set the maximum height of each kanban lane in the main workspace panes, when vertical.

settings.title: Enable Canvas-style Dotted Background for Kanban
settings.description: When enabled, the kanban views will have a canvas-style dotted background.

settings.title: Background Colour (Canvas-style Background)
settings.note2: (Requires 'Enable Canvas-style Dotted Background for Kanban' enabled)
settings.description: This option allows one to choose one of the global accent and background colours for the canvas-style background.

settings.title: Dot Colour (Canvas-style Background)
settings.note2: (Requires 'Enable Canvas-style Dotted Background for Kanban' enabled)
settings.description: This option allows one to choose one of the global accent and background colours for the dots in the canvas-style background.

settings.title: Dot Size (Canvas-style Background)
settings.note2: (Requires 'Enable Canvas-style Dotted Background for Kanban' enabled)
settings.description: Set the size of the dots in the canvas-style background.

settings.title: Dot Spacing (Canvas-style Background)
settings.note2: (Requires 'Enable Canvas-style Dotted Background for Kanban' enabled)
settings.description: Set the spacing of the dots in the canvas-style background.

settings.title: Remove Willemstad-Set Templater Tweaks
settings.description: Willemstad by default adds certain tweaks to the Templater plugin, including setting the same font-type and the disabling of text decoration for non-Templater items inline within a series of Templater code. When enabled, these tweaks will be removed.

settings.title: Remove Willemstad-Set Toolbar Tweaks
settings.description: By default, Willemstad tweaks the Advanced Tables toolbar to remove the prefixed explanations and the Advanced Tables toolbar title. This option allows you to remove these tweaks and restore the plugin's original toolbar appearance.

settings.title: Toolbar Justification
settings.description: This option allows you to change the justification of the Advanced Tables Toolbar

settings.title: Toolbar Title Justification
settings.description: This option allows you to change the justification of the Advanced Tables Toolbar

settings.title: Remove Half-Hour Demarcation Lines
settings.description: The Full Calendar plugin has half-hour lines by default. When this option is enabled, the half-hour demarcation lines will be removed.

settings.title: Use Original Excalidraw Colour Styling
settings.description: Willemstad changes the default Excalidraw colour styling to match the theme. When enabled, the original Excalidraw colour styling will be used.

settings.title: Use Non-Coloured Titlebar Styling
settings.description: Willemstad changes the titlebar colour to match the theme. This means that the titlebar colour for Hover Editor also changes, whilst 'Use Accent Colour for Titlebar' is enabled. When this option is enabled, the original tab bar colour will be used instead.

settings.title: Supporting Willemstad
settings.description: As a matter of principle, I have not, do not, and will not accept monetary compensation/donations of any kind for Willemstad, as my intention has always been to create something that works for me and contribute back to the community. In the event you would like to support Willemstad, please spread the word about the theme, and star the GitHub repository, accessible on the button to the right. Should you still wish to contribute monetarily, please direct your funds elsewhere to other Obsidian community members and theme developers in this list, all of which have contributed to Willemstad and/or the Obsidian theme development scene in one way or another. [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/tingmelvin/willemstad-x)

settings.title: Chris Grieser (@pseudometa)
settings.description: Theme developer of Shimmering Focus and various plugins for Obsidian and Alfred, and Obsidian's resident minimalist; Willemstad's original form as a snippet was built upon SF, and pretty much Willemstad's existence (and quite a number of its early code and its support for Longform) can be attributed to Chris [Ko-Fi](https://ko-fi.com/pseudometa) [GitHub](https://github.com/chrisgrieser/)

settings.title: Damian Korcz
settings.description: Theme developer of Prism, and Maintainer of the Alternative Checkboxes Reference Set; for contributing input to the No-Shadow Sliding Panes and literally saving weeks of my time by introducing and teaching me how to compile SASS/SCSS (if not, Willemstad would be totally unmaintainable since it consists of almost 130 SCSS files and way too many mixins); almost every wacky idea in early Willemstad has roots to him [BuyMeACoffee](https://www.buymeacoffee.com/DamianKorcz) [GitHub](https://github.com/damiankorcz)

settings.title: Cecilia May
settings.description: Theme developer of Primary and pretty much Obsidian's purveyor of colours; whom I co-wrote the Varying/Readable Underlines code with [Ko-Fi](https://ko-fi.com/ceciliamay) [GitHub](https://github.com/ceciliamay)

settings.title: SlRvB
settings.description: Theme developer of ITS Theme; the brains behind a lot of the wonderful things now seen in Obsidian themes; for the Dataview Word Wraps solutions [Ko-Fi](https://ko-fi.com/slrvb) [GitHub](https://github.com/SlRvB)

settings.title: Stephan Ango (@kepano)
settings.description: Theme developer of Minimal (and Flexoki) and Obsidian's CEO; for writing Minimal Cards and Grids previously included in Willemstad, and for the Flexoki colour scheme now supported by Willemstad in its base tones and syntax highlighting [BuyMeACoffee](https://www.buymeacoffee.com/kepano) [GitHub](https://github.com/kepano)

settings.title: @jdanielmourao
settings.description: Theme developer of Sanctum; for having written the original theme I used in Obsidian whilst studying at the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine and for hearing my constant lamentations about how he changed something up in Sanctum's early days that was breaking my workflow [Ko-fi](https://ko-fi.com/jdanielmourao) [GitHub](https://github.com/jdanielmourao)

settings.title: Zhang Chenyu (@zcysxy/Atlas)
settings.description: Theme developer of Terminal; For writing the Multi-Colour Highlighting code that was adapted by Willemstad in its very early days [Ko-Fi](https://ko-fi.com/zcysxy) [GitHub](https://github.com/zcysxy)

settings.title: Matthew Meyers (@mgmeyers)
settings.description: Now one of Obsidian's developers, also theme developer of California Coast, and developer of various plugins in Obsidian, including Kanban and the very cherished Style Settings plugin [BuyMeACoffee](https://www.buymeacoffee.com/mgme) [GitHub](https://github.com/chetachiezikeuzor)

settings.title: Chetachi Ezikeuzor (@chetachi)
settings.description: Theme developer of Yin and Yang; for popularising Base64 font encoding amongst theme developers in Obsidian [Ko-Fi](https://ko-fi.com/chetachi) [GitHub](https://github.com/chetachiezikeuzor)

settings.title: Special Thanks
settings.description: Willemstad also gives its special thanks to Rasmus Andersson (Inter font), Mikhail Sharanda (Manrope font), Colophon Foundry (DM Sans and DM Mono fonts), Jacques Le Bailly/Sebastian Kosch (Crimson Pro font), Nate Baldwin et al. (Leonardo), Blake Robert Mills (MetBrewer), Jimmy Cheung et al. (RemixIcons), Tobias Fried (Phosphor Icons), Cole Bemis et al. (Lucide Icons). Where available, their GitHub repositories are linked here. [](https://github.com/rsms/inter) [](https://github.com/adobe/leonardo) [](https://github.com/BlakeRMills/MetBrewer) [](https://github.com/Remix-Design/RemixIcon) [](https://github.com/phosphor-icons/homepage) [](https://github.com/lucide-icons/lucide)

settings.title: Theme Showcase
settings.description: This segment is reserved for showcasing themes beyond Willemstad, which have pushed the boundaries of what is possible with CSS in Obsidian. Stay tuned for more!

settings.title: Supporting Obsidian
settings.description: Last but not least, you can also choose to support Obsidian by purchasing a Catalyst license, or by subscribing to Obsidian Publish and/or Obsidian Sync. [Catalyst](https://obsidian.md/account/catalyst) [Sync](https://obsidian.md/sync) [Publish](https://obsidian.md/publish)

settings.title: Immersive Editing Modes (Focused Mode and Super Focused Mode)
settings.description: Styles that hide UI elements and fade out non-active lines to help you focus on writing.

settings.title: Note on Immersive Editing Modes
settings.description: Please note that the immersive editing modes (Focused and Super Focused Modes) can be set via hotkeys and is accessible via the Command Palette. Please see the Obsidian's documentation (accessible via the button on the right) to do this. Immersive Editing Modes takes inspiration from [Ryan McQuen's Focus Mode plugin](https://github.com/ryanpcmcquen/obsidian-focus-mode), which Willemstad's maintainer used to use extensively years ago. [Obsidian Docs](https://help.obsidian.md/hotkeys)

settings.title: Focused Mode
settings.description: Hides all UI elements (status bar, sidebars, etc.) except for the tab header bar, and fades out lines of text that are not near your active line. Please note that this setting can be set to a hotkey, and is available via your Command Palette.

settings.title: Super Focused Mode
settings.description: An even more extreme focus mode. Hides all elements, except the current active page. Does not require 'Focused Mode' enabled. Please note that this setting can be set to a hotkey, and is available via your Command Palette.

settings.title: Distant Line Opacity
settings.description: Controls the minimum opacity for lines far away from your active line in Focused Mode. Default is 0.2.

settings.title: Near Line Fade Factor
settings.description: Controls the opacity of the lines fade out as they get further from the active line. Higher values mean a bigger difference in opacity, which is stepped by the value you input here, multiplied by the amount of lines away from the active line it is (up to the third line). The default is 0.15.

settings.title: Display CSSClass
settings.description: Transform your notes into beautiful LaTeX-style documents. Creates academic paper styling with proper typography, equation numbering, and LaTeX-like formatting for professional document presentation. To use LaTeX Display CSSClass, type `cssclasses: latex` in the document's YAML.

settings.title: Documentation for LaTeX Display CSSClass
settings.description: [Example](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Global+CSSClass/LaTeX+Display+Example) [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Global+CSSClass/LaTeX+Display)

settings.title: Font Size
settings.description: Sets the base font size for the LaTeX document style.

settings.title: Reading Font Family
settings.description: Font family used for reading mode. Supports multiple fallbacks.

settings.title: Source/Editor Font Family
settings.description: Font family used in source/editor mode. Supports multiple fallbacks.

settings.title: Paragraph Text Indent
settings.description: Sets the indentation for the first line of paragraphs.

settings.title: Table Font Size (Preview)
settings.description: Font size for tables in reading/preview mode.

settings.title: Table Font Size (Editor)
settings.description: Font size for tables in editor/live preview mode.

settings.title: Equation Number Format
settings.description: Format for equation numbering.

settings.title: Preserve Theme Background Colour in LaTeX CSSClass PDF Exports
settings.description: By default, LaTeX documents generally should be exported to white backgrounds, and thus this CSSClass overrides the print stylesheet available for the rest of Willemstad. Toggle this to force the LaTeX CSSClass document to take the background colour of the theme when exporting.

settings.title: Cornell Notes CSSClass
settings.description: Transform your notes into the classic Cornell Note-taking format with a two-column layout: a narrow left column for keywords and cues, and a wider right column for detailed content. To use Cornell Notes CSSClass, type `cssclasses: cornell` in the document's YAML.

settings.title: Documentation for Cornell Notes CSSClass
settings.description: [Example](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Global+CSSClass/Cornell+Notes+Example) [Docs](https://willemstad.cc/Features/Global+CSSClass/Cornell+Notes)

settings.title: Minimum Aside Width
settings.description: Sets the minimum pixel width for the aside panel. The aside will not shrink below this size.

settings.title: Preferred Aside Width
settings.description: Use this setting to change the preferred percentage width of the left aside panel.

settings.title: Gap Between Left and Right Content
settings.description: Use this setting to change the gap between the left and right content of the Cornell Notes CSSClass.

settings.title: Opacidad del fondo
settings.description: Controla la transparencia de la aplicación

settings.title: Efecto Pseudo Mica
settings.description: Habilita el efecto de vidrio esmerilado (requiere fondo translúcido)

settings.title: Intensidad del desenfoque
settings.description: Controla la intensidad del efecto de desenfoque

settings.title: Color de acento
settings.description: Color principal del tema

settings.title: Tamaño de fuente
settings.description: Tamaño base de la fuente

settings.title: Altura de línea
settings.description: Espaciado entre líneas

settings.title: Radio de bordes
settings.description: Redondez de los elementos

settings.title: Colores de carpetas
settings.description: Habilitar colores en las carpetas

settings.title: Accent Color
settings.description: For links and interactive elements.

settings.title: Base Color
settings.description: Change base hue and saturation.

settings.title: Custom Fonts
settings.description: For customizing theme fonts.

settings.title: UI Font
settings.description: Font used for the user interface
settings.default: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Microsoft YaHei Light", sans-serif

settings.title: Editor Font

settings.title: Headers in Editor Font
settings.description: Font used for the headers in editor
settings.default: Inter, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif, "Apple Color Emoji", "Segoe UI Emoji", "Segoe UI Symbol", "Microsoft YaHei Light", sans-serif

settings.title: Monospace Font
settings.description: Used for code blocks, front matter, etc

settings.title: Typography
settings.description: Extra typographical features.

settings.title: WYSIWYG Headings in Editor
settings.description: Adds WYSIWYG-like functionality for headings in editor

settings.title: Enhanced Editor Typography
settings.description: Adds enhanced WYSIWYG-like functionality in editor

settings.title: Rainbow Headers
settings.description: Adds rainbow colors for headers

settings.title: Readable Line Width
settings.description: The maximum line width in rem units for readable line width

settings.title: Body Line Height
settings.description: Line height of the main text, expects a decimal value

settings.title: Highlight Color
settings.description: This will change the color of highlights in light and dark mode

settings.title: Heading Changes
settings.description: Editing headings individually.

settings.title: Change Heading Fonts
settings.description: This will allow for editing of headings individually.

settings.title: Change Heading Sizes
settings.description: This will allow for editing of headings individually.

settings.description: This will allow for editing of headings individually.

settings.title: Minimalize Embedded Notes
settings.description: This will minimalize the look of embed notes

settings.description: This will remove code languages from code blocks.

settings.description: This will remove the relationship lines from your notes in preview mode

settings.description: This will remove the relationship lines from your notes in editor mode

settings.description: This will allow you to set a custom line indent value

settings.title: Change the Relationship Line Thickness in Preview

settings.title: Change the Relationship Line Thickness in Editor
settings.description: This will allow you to set a custom relationship line thickness in editor

settings.title: Remove Custom Icons
settings.description: This will remove all custom icons in the file explorer (will also improve scrolling performance).

settings.title: Show Folder Icons
settings.description: This will display folder icons (set to none by default for those that wish to use their own icons)

settings.title: Remove Text Reverse on Attachments
settings.description: This will undo text reverse of attachments and remove attachments icons

settings.title: Remove Status Bar
settings.description: This will hide the status bar and you can hover to see it

settings.title: Remove Status Bar Icon
settings.description: This will remove the ☯️ symbol from the status bar

settings.title: More Customization
settings.description: Extra customizable features.

settings.title: File Explorer Relationship Lines
settings.description: This will add relationship lines to files and folders in file explorer

settings.title: Set background customization for all side panes
settings.description: Allows customization of backgrounds for all panes. Simply edit the image url within the "url()" function.

settings.title: Set background for specific panes
settings.description: Allows customization of backgrounds for specific panes. Simply edit the image url within the "url()" function.

settings.title: MacOS-Like Transparent Window
settings.description: This will give your workspace a MacOS-like translucency

settings.title: Black mobile background
settings.description: Change mobile editor background to default theme black

settings.title: Disable mobile floating-action button
settings.description: Revert placement of edit/preview button to default in header (mobile)

settings.description: Created with ❤︎ by Gixo. Forked from Things theme.

settings.title: Window Settings
settings.description: Adjust the window settings. Affects all parts except the editor area.
settings.description.zh: 调整窗体设置，影响除编辑器区外的所有部分。

settings.title: Brightness of window.
settings.description: Adjust the brightness of the entire form。
settings.description.zh: 调整整个窗体的亮度。

settings.title: Adjust the font of the Window.
settings.description: Adjust the font size of some parts, including the document directory on the left, the document title on the top, the outline directory on the right, the tag list, and so on.
settings.description.zh: 调节部分字体大小，包含左侧文档目录、顶部文档标题、右侧大纲目录、标签列表等等。

settings.title: Close left vault profile.
settings.title.zh: 将仓库切换放置为默认底部
settings.description: Switch the vault profile to the default bottom.
settings.description.zh: 将仓库切换放置为默认底部。新版改为在底部，不太喜欢，这里调整为原来的左侧。

settings.title: Editor Settings
settings.description: Adjust the editor settings. Affects only the editor area.
settings.description.zh: 调整编辑器设置，影响编辑器区。

settings.title: Hide the Heading prompt in the editor.
settings.title.zh: 隐藏编辑器中标题提示
settings.description: Hide the h1...h2... and other small title prompts in front of headings at all levels.
settings.description.zh: 隐藏各级标题前面的 h1...h2...等小标题提示

settings.title: Hide the Heading bottom line in the editor.
settings.title.zh: 隐藏编辑器中各级标题下的虚线
settings.description: Hide the h1...h2... and other small title bottom line in headings at all levels.
settings.description.zh: 隐藏各级标题前面的 h1...h2...等小标题下的线虚

settings.title: Open text justification.
settings.description: Default text is left-aligned. If it is turned on, it is justified.
settings.description.zh: 默认文本左对齐，打开则两端对齐。

settings.title: Adjust the width of the editor.
settings.description: Adjust the editor width. The default is 800. The minimum is 500 and the maximum is 2000.
settings.description.zh: 调整编辑器宽度，默认为 800，最小 500，最大 2000。

settings.title: The gap of the text paragraph.
settings.description: Adjust the gap of the text paragraph.
settings.description.zh: 调整文本段间距。

settings.title: The height of the text line.
settings.description: Adjust the height of the text line.
settings.description.zh: 调整文本行高。

settings.title: Brightness of editor.
settings.description: Adjust the brightness of the middle editor area.
settings.description.zh: 调整中间编辑器区的亮度。

settings.title: Windows/Linux
settings.description: Only valid for Windows/Linux.
settings.description.zh: 仅对Windows/Linux 有效。

settings.title: Close the translucent window.
settings.description: Turn off the window translucency of Windows/Linux. It is only effective for Windows/Linux.
settings.description.zh: 关闭Windows/Linux的窗口半透明，仅对Windows/Linux有效

settings.title: Transparency of window background
settings.description: Adjust the transparency of the form background on the Windows/Linux platform. Only effective for Windows/Linux. Recommended values：For dark colors：0.2. For light colors：0.15.
settings.description.zh: 调整 Windows/Linux 平台的窗体背景的透明度。仅对 Windows/Linux 有效。推荐值：暗色：0.2 亮色：0.15

settings.title: Brightness of form background.
settings.description: Adjust the brightness of the form background on the Windows/Linux platform. Only effective for Windows/Linux. Recommended values：0.9.
settings.description.zh: 调整 Windows/Linux 平台的窗体背景的亮度。仅对 Windows/Linux 有效。推荐值：0.9

settings.title: the background picture of the window
settings.description: Set the form background image; fill the image URL between the quotes; leave blank for no background image by default.
settings.description.zh: 设置窗体背景图片；将图片地址填入''号中间；若不填则默认无背景图片

settings.title: Adjust the color of the middle editing area.
settings.title.zh: 调整中间编辑区的颜色

settings.title: macOS
settings.description: Only valid for macOS.
settings.description.zh: macOS 有效。

settings.title: MacOS：The transparency of the window background.
settings.description: Adjust the transparency of the window background on the Mac platform. Only effective for macOS, and the translucent effect switch is turned on in Settings -> Appearance.
settings.description.zh: 调整 mac 平台的窗体背景的透明度。仅对 macOS 有效，且在设置 -> 外观中打开了半透明效果开关

settings.title: Adjust the color of the middle editing area.
settings.title.zh: 调整中间编辑区的颜色
